summary: Completed deterministic Alpha succession resolution: abdication-like succession triggers, peaceful/regency/disputed resolution consequences, M6 legitimacy source/profile integration, disputed recognized-order blocking, explicit protocol/save trigger schemas, event-tail save/load support, and focused property/invariant/save rejection coverage.

tests_run.result: Initial red tests failed as expected: abdicated protocol payload rejected; abdication command rejected; succession resolution lacked M6 legitimacy; disputed succession was not a recognized-order blocker; succession save round-trip rejected.
Diagnostic run exposed save/load rejection from unsupported sim.m3-succession-updated event-tail entries.
24 files / 184 tests passed. Node emitted the existing MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning for tests/fixtures/deterministic-rng-worker.ts.
Node/worker hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, M4 232f62ab, M5 bf042e42.
Architecture boundaries passed; sim determinism passed; root Vitest 37 files / 285 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file / 4 tests passed.
First run failed during sim-core typecheck because nextNumericId expected rows with id while M6 legitimacy sources use sourceId.
format:check, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture:check passed.

decisions: Kept succession under the existing GameCommand path: sim.record-character-status now accepts status abdicated and sim.resolve-succession remains the only resolution command.
Represented abdication-like scenario triggers as explicit M3 succession trigger kind abdication in protocol/save/sim-core schemas without increasing schemaVersion.

On successful succession resolution, reused the M6 legitimacy source/profile substrate instead of duplicating a legitimacy system; sources use sourceKind succession-continuity and sourceRef m3.succession.<id>.
Mapped peaceful succession to +700 legitimacy, regency to +300, and disputed succession to -700 for vassal-rulers; disputed resolved crises remain recognized-order blockers.
Added save/load event-tail parsing for sim.m3-succession-updated so auditable succession events survive save round-trip.
risks: R4/R3 boundary: this changes protocol, save DTOs, authoritative state validation, event-tail parsing, and recognized-order victory gating; systems_architect should review schema compatibility and whether abdication should require a future schemaVersion bump.
Legitimacy magnitudes are deterministic Alpha constants, not balanced final values; balance review may tune them later.
Regency is accepted as an M3/M6 Alpha temporary stewardship outcome because the existing M3 state machine already modeled regency; no separate stewardship office system was introduced.
requested_action: Review actual diff for architecture, determinism, save/protocol schema compatibility, scope compliance, and test adequacy.
